mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of unemployment and payroll growth triggers recession risk premiums, causing institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-series unemployment baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in residential construction and consumer credit indicates strengthening household balance sheets, which supports durable goods demand and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when both housing starts and consumer credit expand concurrently, or the interaction term loses statistical significance after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, rising industrial production reflects sustained capacity utilization and pricing power, which directly supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while equity returns remain negative for more than 50% of observations, indicating that supply growth is decoupled from earnings realization.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals a demand contraction that forces analysts to downgrade earnings estimates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for over 60% of quarters following sub-1.5% GDP growth, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month payroll growth sustains consumer spending expectations, providing a positive drift to equity prices over short horizons as earnings revisions remain anchored to baseline growth.
failure_condition: Short-term forward returns are negative or zero when payroll growth exceeds the 150k monthly threshold, or the signal fails to generate positive Rank IC over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, triggering stagflation fears that compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when inflation outpaces the policy rate for consecutive months, or the interaction effect disappears after controlling for oil price shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding real GDP signal broad-based economic acceleration, which improves corporate revenue visibility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative when both housing starts and GDP expand simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to outperform a GDP-only baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.
